questions.question: Why is move generation correctness a first milestone in chess engine development?
options: It is optional for basic play
Search quality is meaningless if legal moves are wrong
It only affects the UI display
It replaces the evaluation function
questions.explanation: If the move generator produces illegal moves or misses legal ones, every search result is suspect. Perft testing (counting legal moves at known depths) verifies move generator correctness before search is even added.

questions.question: What does the minimax algorithm assume about both players?
options: Both players make random moves
Both players play optimally, maximizing/minimizing the evaluation score
Only one player has a strategy
The game is always a draw
questions.explanation: Minimax assumes the opponent plays optimally. At each level, the side to move picks the move that maximizes its score; the opponent picks the move that minimizes it. This guarantees finding the best move against a perfect opponent.

questions.question: What does alpha-beta pruning improve?
options: Board rendering quality
Search efficiency by skipping provably irrelevant branches
Network communication speed
Rule complexity
questions.explanation: Alpha-beta maintains two bounds: alpha (best score the maximizer can guarantee) and beta (best score the minimizer can guarantee). When alpha >= beta, the current branch can't improve the result and is pruned. In the best case, this doubles the searchable depth.

questions.question: Why start with simple material-only evaluation?
options: To maximize final engine strength immediately
To establish a stable baseline before adding positional complexity
To avoid writing tests
To remove the search entirely
questions.explanation: A simple evaluation (just material counting) lets you verify that the search works correctly. If the engine hangs pieces with material-only eval, the bug is in search or move generation, not evaluation. Adding positional terms later is easier when the baseline is stable.

questions.question: What is perft testing and why is it essential?
options: Performance benchmarking of the search
Counting legal moves at known depths to verify move generator correctness
Testing the evaluation function
Measuring memory usage
questions.explanation: Perft(pos, depth) counts the total number of legal move sequences up to the given depth. Known perft values for standard positions (like the starting position) are published. If your perft counts match, your move generator is correct.

_meta.title: Build a Chess Engine with Search & Eval
